title: Saptapadi Stuns Audiences at Prithvi Theatre
excerpt: Manoj Shah's latest directorial venture is a tour de force of emotional storytelling, with Neha Joshi delivering a career-best performance.

title: A Definitive Adhe Adhure for Our Times
excerpt: Ideas Unlimited's revival of Mohan Rakesh's classic feels startlingly contemporary, anchored by powerhouse performances.

title: Kaumudi: A Love Letter to Gujarati Theatre
excerpt: Manoj Shah returns to his Gujarati roots with a sweeping historical epic that rivals anything on the Hindi stage.

title: 35 Years of Fearless Theatre: The Ideas Unlimited Story
excerpt: From a small experimental group to one of India's most respected theatre companies, the journey of Ideas Unlimited Productions is a testament to artistic conviction.

title: Manoj Shah: The Quiet Revolutionary of Indian Theatre
excerpt: In a rare interview, the founder of Ideas Unlimited Productions reflects on three decades of pushing boundaries.

title: Tagore ni Rachana nu Gujarati Connection
excerpt: The only Gujarati play presented at the five-day Tagore Festival in Kolkata — Manoj Shah's Achalayatan explores the rigidity pervading religion and education through Tagore's most allegorical work. Shah describes the play's tone as one of self-discovery rather than opposition to systems.

title: Adbhut: A Gujarati Play That Makes You Feel Again
excerpt: RJ Devaki's solo performance dissolves the boundary between stage and audience, turning strangers into co-performers in an interactive exploration of mental health that leaves no one untouched.

title: Blast From The Past: New Hindi Play Revolves Around Bhamasha
excerpt: Whether Shah Jahan, Ashoka, or Bhamasha — Indian history is full of intriguing tales. Veteran actor Dayashankar Pandey brings the 16th-century Jain warrior to life, with folk musician Chugge Khan and his Rajasthani ensemble providing the score.

title: History Comes Alive On Stage
excerpt: This Republic Day, catch a Hindi play that joys tribute to Bhamasha, advisor to Maharana Pratap who donated all his wealth to Mewar to aid the war against the Mughals.

title: A Colorful, Wilting Bloom
excerpt: The 120-minute staging brings out the highs and lows of Ruttie's life, her love for and obsession with Jinnah, her later dependence on drink and opium, her solitary wanderings in Europe and her loneliness. Bhamini Oza Gandhi shines as Ruttie.

title: 41 Varsh ni Vay na Mohammed Ali Jinnah e Matra 18 Varsh ni Ruttie Sathe Lagna Karya
excerpt: A detailed account of the Ruttie Petit-Jinnah story and Ideas Unlimited's Bombay Flower — the Gujarati play that brings this forgotten chapter of pre-Partition history to the stage.

title: Why Mughal-e-Azam: The Musical Matters
excerpt: The ambitious musical adaptation proves that Indian theatre can match Broadway in scale and spectacle.
